content:
First note that 7, 11, and 13 are pairwise coprime, and 7·11·13 = 1001.  By CRT, asking for 
   n² ≡ r  (mod 7),
   n² ≡ r  (mod 11),
   n² ≡ r  (mod 13)
simultaneously is equivalent to asking for 
   n² ≡ r  (mod 1001),
with the extra constraint r∈{0,…,6} (since the residue must lie in each of 0,…,6,0,…,10 and 0,…,12).

(1)  For a prime p, the equation x²≡r(mod p) has
      • 1 solution if r≡0(mod p),
      • 2 solutions if r≠0 is a quadratic residue mod p,
      • 0 solutions otherwise.

Compute quadratic residues of 7,11,13 restricted to r=0,…,6:

p=7: QR={0,1,2,4}  
p=11: QR={0,1,3,4,5,9}  
p=13: QR={0,1,3,4,9,10,12}

r=0: 1·1·1=1 solution mod 1001  
r=1: 2·2·2=8 solutions  
r=2,3,5,6: each fails at least one prime ⇒ 0 solutions  
r=4: 2·2·2=8 solutions

Hence there are 1+8+8 = 17 solutions mod 1001.  Call them the residue‐classes

   A = {0}   (r=0), 
   B = the 8 classes with n²≡1 (r=1),
   C = the 8 classes with n²≡4 (r=4).

(2)  We want 1≤n<2018.  Since 2018=2·1001+16, each of these 17 residue‐classes appears twice in 1≤n≤2001, and *some* appear a third time among n=2002,…,2017 (those whose least‐positive representative a lies in 1,…,15).  Checking a=1,…,15, one finds only a=1 (from B) and a=2 (from C) are indeed solutions.  Thus

– all 17 classes give 2 hits in [1,2001], for 2·17=34 total  
– the two special classes a=1,2 give one extra each in [2002,2017],

for a grand total of 34+2 = 36.  

Answer: \boxed{36}.

with the extra constraint r∈{0,…,6} (since the residue must lie in each of 0,…,6,0,…,10 and 0,…,12).

      • 0 solutions otherwise.

Compute quadratic residues of 7,11,13 restricted to r=0,…,6:

p=7: QR={0,1,2,4}  
p=11: QR={0,1,3,4,5,9}  
p=13: QR={0,1,3,4,9,10,12}

r=0: 1·1·1=1 solution mod 1001  
r=1: 2·2·2=8 solutions  
r=2,3,5,6: each fails at least one prime ⇒ 0 solutions  
r=4: 2·2·2=8 solutions

   A = {0}   (r=0), 
   B = the 8 classes with n²≡1 (r=1),
   C = the 8 classes with n²≡4 (r=4).

– the two special classes a=1,2 give one extra each in [2002,2017],

for a grand total of 34+2 = 36.
